children.heading: Allowance of credit
children.label: a Allowance of credit
children.text: There shall be allowed as a credit against the tax imposed by this chapter for the taxable year with respect to each qualifying child of the taxpayer for which the taxpayer is allowed a deduction under section 151 an amount equal to $1,000.

children.heading: Limitation based on adjusted gross income
children.label: 1 Limitation based on adjusted gross income
children.text: The amount of the credit allowable under subsection (a) shall be reduced (but not below zero) by $50 for each $1,000 (or fraction thereof) by which the taxpayer’s modified adjusted gross income exceeds the threshold amount. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “modified adjusted gross income” means adjusted gross income increased by any amount excluded from gross income under section 911, 931, or 933.

children.label: A
children.text: $110,000 in the case of a joint return,

children.label: B
children.text: $75,000 in the case of an individual who is not married, and

children.label: C
children.text: $55,000 in the case of a married individual filing a separate return.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “qualifying child” means a qualifying child of the taxpayer (as defined in section 152(c)) who has not attained age 17.

children.heading: Exception for certain noncitizens
children.label: 2 Exception for certain noncitizens
children.text: The term “qualifying child” shall not include any individual who would not be a dependent if subparagraph (A) of section 152(b)(3) were applied without regard to all that follows “resident of the United States”.

children.label: A
children.text: the credit which would be allowed under this section without regard to this subsection and the limitation under section 26(a) or

children.label: i
children.text: 15 percent of so much of the taxpayer’s earned income (within the meaning of section 32) which is taken into account in computing taxable income for the taxable year as exceeds $3,000, or

children.label: I
children.text: the taxpayer’s social security taxes for the taxable year, over

children.label: II
children.text: the credit allowed under section 32 for the taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of the taxes imposed by sections 3101 and 3201(a) on amounts received by the taxpayer during the calendar year in which the taxable year begins,

children.label: ii
children.text: 50 percent of the taxes imposed by section 1401 on the self-employment income of the taxpayer for the taxable year, and

children.label: iii
children.text: 50 percent of the taxes imposed by section 3211(a) on amounts received by the taxpayer during the calendar year in which the taxable year begins.

children.heading: Coordination with special refund of social security taxes
children.label: B Coordination with special refund of social security taxes
children.text: The term “social security taxes” shall not include any taxes to the extent the taxpayer is entitled to a special refund of such taxes under section 6413(c).

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: C Special rule
children.text: Any amounts paid pursuant to an agreement under section 3121() (relating to agreements entered into by American employers with respect to foreign affiliates) which are equivalent to the taxes referred to in subparagraph (A)(i) shall be treated as taxes referred to in such subparagraph.l

children.heading: Exception for taxpayers excluding foreign earned income
children.label: 3 Exception for taxpayers excluding foreign earned income
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to any taxpayer for any taxable year if such taxpayer elects to exclude any amount from gross income under section 911 for such taxable year.

children.heading: Qualifying child identification requirement
children.label: 1 Qualifying child identification requirement
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under this section to a taxpayer with respect to any qualifying child unless the taxpayer includes the name and taxpayer identification number of such qualifying child on the return of tax for the taxable year and such taxpayer identification number was issued on or before the due date for filing such return.

children.heading: Taxpayer identification requirement
children.label: 2 Taxpayer identification requirement
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under this section if the taxpayer identification number of the taxpayer was issued after the due date for filing the return for the taxable year.

children.heading: Taxable year must be full taxable year
children.label: f Taxable year must be full taxable year
children.text: Except in the case of a taxable year closed by reason of the death of the taxpayer, no credit shall be allowable under this section in the case of a taxable year covering a period of less than 12 months.

children.heading: Restrictions on taxpayers who improperly claimed credit in prior year
children.label: g Restrictions on taxpayers who improperly claimed credit in prior year

children.heading: Taxpayers making prior fraudulent or reckless claims
children.label: 1 Taxpayers making prior fraudulent or reckless claims

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under this section for any taxable year in the disallowance period.

children.label: i
children.text: the period of 10 taxable years after the most recent taxable year for which there was a final determination that the taxpayer’s claim of credit under this section was due to fraud, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the period of 2 taxable years after the most recent taxable year for which there was a final determination that the taxpayer’s claim of credit under this section was due to reckless or intentional disregard of rules and regulations (but not due to fraud).

children.heading: Taxpayers making improper prior claims
children.label: 2 Taxpayers making improper prior claims
children.text: In the case of a taxpayer who is denied credit under this section for any taxable year as a result of the deficiency procedures under subchapter B of chapter 63, no credit shall be allowed under this section for any subsequent taxable year unless the taxpayer provides such information as the Secretary may require to demonstrate eligibility for such credit.

children.heading: Special rules for taxable years beginning after 2017
children.label: h Special rules for taxable years beginning after 2017

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of a taxable year beginning after , this section shall be applied as provided in paragraphs (2) through (7).December 31, 2017

children.heading: Credit amount
children.label: 2 Credit amount
children.text: Subsection (a) shall be applied by substituting “$2,200” for “$1,000”.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 3 Limitation
children.text: In lieu of the amount determined under subsection (b)(2), the threshold amount shall be $400,000 in the case of a joint return ($200,000 in any other case).

children.heading: Partial credit allowed for certain other dependents
children.label: 4 Partial credit allowed for certain other dependents

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The credit determined under subsection (a) (after the application of paragraph (2)) shall be increased by $500 for each dependent of the taxpayer (as defined in section 152) other than a qualifying child described in subsection (c).

children.heading: Exception for certain noncitizens
children.label: B Exception for certain noncitizens
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply with respect to any individual who would not be a dependent if subparagraph (A) of section 152(b)(3) were applied without regard to all that follows “resident of the United States”.

children.heading: Certain qualifying children
children.label: C Certain qualifying children
children.text: In the case of any qualifying child with respect to whom a credit is not allowed under this section by reason of paragraph (7), such child shall be treated as a dependent to whom subparagraph (A) applies.

children.heading: Maximum amount of refundable credit
children.label: 5 Maximum amount of refundable credit
children.text: The amount determined under subsection (d)(1)(A) with respect to any qualifying child shall not exceed $1,400, and such subsection shall be applied without regard to paragraph (4) of this subsection.

children.heading: Earned income threshold for refundable credit
children.label: 6 Earned income threshold for refundable credit
children.text: Subsection (d)(1)(B)(i) shall be applied by substituting “$2,500” for “$3,000”.

children.label: i
children.text: the taxpayer’s social security number (or, in the case of a joint return, the social security number of at least 1 spouse), and

children.label: ii
children.text: the social security number of such qualifying child.

children.label: i
children.text: to a citizen of the United States or pursuant to subclause (I) (or that portion of subclause (III) that relates to subclause (I)) of section 205(c)(2)(B)(i) of the Social Security Act, and

children.label: ii
children.text: before the due date for such return.

children.label: A
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year in which the taxable year begins, determined by substituting “2017” for “2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.heading: Special rule for adjustment of credit amount
children.label: 2 Special rule for adjustment of credit amount
A
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year in which the taxable year begins, determined by substituting “2024” for “2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.heading: Rounding
children.label: 3 Rounding
children.text: If any increase under this subsection is not a multiple of $100, such increase shall be rounded to the next lowest multiple of $100.

children.heading: Reconciliation of credit and advance credit
children.label: j Reconciliation of credit and advance credit

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount of the credit allowed under this section to any taxpayer for any taxable year shall be reduced (but not below zero) by the aggregate amount of payments made under section 7527A to such taxpayer during such taxable year. Any failure to so reduce the credit shall be treated as arising out of a mathematical or clerical error and assessed according to section 6213(b)(1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the aggregate amount of payments under section 7527A to the taxpayer during the taxable year exceeds the amount of the credit allowed under this section to such taxpayer for such taxable year (determined without regard to paragraph (1)), the tax imposed by this chapter for such taxable year shall be increased by the amount of such excess. Any failure to so increase the tax shall be treated as arising out of a mathematical or clerical error and assessed according to section 6213(b)(1).

children.heading: Safe harbor based on modified adjusted gross income
children.label: B Safe harbor based on modified adjusted gross income

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of a taxpayer whose modified adjusted gross income (as defined in subsection (b)) for the taxable year does not exceed 200 percent of the applicable income threshold, the amount of the increase determined under subparagraph (A) with respect to such taxpayer for such taxable year shall be reduced (but not below zero) by the safe harbor amount.

children.heading: Phase out of safe harbor amount
children.label: ii Phase out of safe harbor amount
children.text: In the case of a taxpayer whose modified adjusted gross income (as defined in subsection (b)) for the taxable year exceeds the applicable income threshold, the safe harbor amount otherwise in effect under clause (i) shall be reduced by the amount which bears the same ratio to such amount as such excess bears to the applicable income threshold.

children.label: I
children.text: $60,000 in the case of a joint return or surviving spouse (as defined in section 2(a)),

children.label: II
children.text: $50,000 in the case of a head of household, and

children.label: III
children.text: $40,000 in any other case.

children.label: I
children.text: $2,000, multiplied by

children.label: II
children.text: the excess (if any) of the number of qualified children taken into account in determining the annual advance amount with respect to the taxpayer under section 7527A with respect to months beginning in such taxable year, over the number of qualified children taken into account in determining the credit allowed under this section for such taxable year.

children.heading: Application of credit in possessions
children.label: k Application of credit in possessions

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall pay to each possession of the United States with a mirror code tax system amounts equal to the loss (if any) to that possession by reason of the application of this section (determined without regard to this subsection) with respect to taxable years beginning after 2020. Such amounts shall be determined by the Secretary based on information provided by the government of the respective possession.

children.heading: Coordination with credit allowed against United States income taxes
children.label: B Coordination with credit allowed against United States income taxes
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under this section for any taxable year to any individual to whom a credit is allowable against taxes imposed by a possession of the United States with a mirror code tax system by reason of the application of this section in such possession for such taxable year.

children.heading: Mirror code tax system
children.label: C Mirror code tax system
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “mirror code tax system” means, with respect to any possession of the United States, the income tax system of such possession if the income tax liability of the residents of such possession under such system is determined by reference to the income tax laws of the United States as if such possession were the United States.

children.heading: Application to taxable years in 2021
children.label: A Application to taxable years in 2021
i
children.text: For application of refundable credit to residents of Puerto Rico, see subsection (i)(1).

children.label: ii
children.text: For nonapplication of advance payment to residents of Puerto Rico, see section 7527A(e)(4)(A).

children.heading: Application to taxable years after 2021
children.label: B Application to taxable years after 2021
i
children.text: the credit determined under this section shall be allowable to such resident, and

children.label: ii
children.text: subsection (d)(1)(B)(ii) shall be applied without regard to the phrase “in the case of a taxpayer with 3 or more qualifying children”.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall pay to American Samoa amounts estimated by the Secretary as being equal to the aggregate benefits that would have been provided to residents of American Samoa by reason of the application of this section for taxable years beginning after 2020 if the provisions of this section had been in effect in American Samoa (applied as if American Samoa were the United States and without regard to the application of this section to bona fide residents of Puerto Rico under subsection (i)(1)).

children.heading: Distribution requirement
children.label: B Distribution requirement
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply unless American Samoa has a plan, which has been approved by the Secretary, under which American Samoa will promptly distribute such payments to its residents.

children.heading: Coordination with credit allowed against United States income taxes
children.label: C Coordination with credit allowed against United States income taxes

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of a taxable year with respect to which a plan is approved under subparagraph (B), this section (other than this subsection) shall not apply to any individual eligible for a distribution under such plan.

children.heading: Application of section in event of absence of approved plan
children.label: ii Application of section in event of absence of approved plan
I
children.text: if such taxable year begins in 2021, subsection (i)(1) shall be applied by substituting “bona fide resident of Puerto Rico or American Samoa” for “bona fide resident of Puerto Rico”, and

children.label: II
children.text: if such taxable year begins after , rules similar to the rules of paragraph (2)(B) shall apply with respect to bona fide residents of American Samoa (within the meaning of section 937(a)).December 31, 2021

children.heading: Treatment of payments
children.label: 4 Treatment of payments
children.text: For purposes of , United States Code, the payments under this subsection shall be treated in the same manner as a refund due from a credit provision referred to in subsection (b)(2) of such section.section 1324 of title 31
